text: Consensys Says North Korea-Linked Developer Contributed to MetaMask Code Before Access Was Revoked According to Drop Site News, blockchain firm Consensys accidentally hired a software developer linked to North Korea. The developer, using the alias "Tyler Knapp," joined through a third-party service provider and contributed to MetaMask-related code, including crypto-to-fiat conversion features. Consensys said it revoked the developer's access after identifying the security risk and confirmed that no assets or data were compromised, no malicious code was deployed, and users were not affected. — link

text: FTX to Distribute About $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Payout on July 31 FTX will begin its fifth creditor distribution on July 31, paying approximately $900 million to holders of allowed claims in the Convenience and Non-Convenience Classes under its reorganization plan who completed the required pre-distribution steps by the June 16 record date. Eligible creditors are expected to receive funds through their selected distribution provider within one to three business days after July 31. Eligible preferred equity holders will also receive a second payment that day. — link

text: It's been absolute carnage the last few days onchain. Quite surprised how aggressive the rotations from the Robinhood eco were. Attention and conviction is so fickle that a single follow or retweet from Vlad changes the entire meta. Personally I don't see the eco dying this quickly, however there needs to be much better comms from the team within the next week, otherwise traders aren't going to waste time there. Seeing similar sentiment from smart traders expecting some form of a bounce here. Doesn't help that the top 50 holders of Cashcat were sitting on 1M+ PNL, once there was no immediate catalyst it became a no brainer for many to sell. I said in a previous post that early this week would be an important inflection point whether they continue endorsing, so far there's been minimal comms regarding memes and RWA's have taken more of the spotlight + tendies attempted vamp. Ansem looks good around 150-200 mil. I skimmed his recent article, essentially goes further in depth to my thesis I wrote before and I genuinely like the narrative despite it being a creator coin. I was one of the few people who were early to Mitch and the streamer coins, while they failed I do believe there will be a future model that makes that type of asset and utility valuable. So far Ansems the best we've seen by a mile. One thing that's important to note is he was the catalyst that opened peoples minds to higher floors and the revival of memes in the first place, everything that came after was essentially leeching off of his success. Robinhood chain has obviously been in production for a long time, without Ansem I'd doubt they would have leaned so heavily on memes though. I really struggle seeing Cashcat just dying here, the leg downs have been brutal and cutting through every support level. Cashcat at 50 mil and Ansem at 150 mil feel like very similar trades and RR. Honestly I think you could flip a coin for which pulls ahead from here. Ansem seems more structured and a bet on future growth and speculation of crypto influencers, Cashcat is a bet on Vlad flagshipping it as the mascot for retail degeneracy. I do still hold some Cashcat and currently doing mental gymnastics at what price I'd buy Ansem. The only other low cap I'm holding/have some conviction on is $PRIZE. It's the first agentic trading protocol on Robinhood brokerage accounts. A friend of mine is the dev, he was struggling with the marketing and branding, I looked at the product and thought it was cool, so decided to drop everything I was doing the past few days and help with some marketing items. Will see some cool stuff soon. Mcaps only around 400k and it's a Virtuals coin so liquidities ass, DCA in if you're going to buy. I'm not an official team member or tying myself to this so don't buy it with that expectation. Was a cool opportunity to get my hands dirty after being on the sidelines trading for the past 2 years as someone who previously lived a very dense marketing career. Will be back to normal write ups in the coming days, this has been the main reason I haven't been writing as much. Overall heavy fatigue and fragmentation now that Brian and Cobie have decided to participate in the chain wars. While it's very tiring and hard to have conviction on eco's, it does present consistently good entries on coins you believe in. Think the trick is reducing size by 50-75% as these rotations are relentless. You really need to stick to a strong fundamental thesis past the 5 min chart or play the non consensus fresh runner.

text: Coinbase Bitcoin Premium Index Stays Negative for Record 60 Consecutive Days According to Coinglass, the Coinbase Bitcoin Premium Index has remained negative for 60 consecutive days since May 19, with the latest reading at -0.1025%. The previous record was a 40-day negative streak from January 16 to February 24, surpassing the roughly 30-day stretch seen during the Oct. 11 market crash. Historically, prolonged negative premiums have often coincided with weaker U.S. institutional demand, raising concerns over near-term pullback pressure. — link

text: Argentine Judge Freezes 25 Crypto Accounts, Orders Six Exchanges to Identify Account Holders Argentine federal judge Marcelo Martínez de Giorgi ordered 25 crypto accounts linked to the LIBRA investigation frozen and directed Binance, Bybit, OKX, CoinEx, FixedFloat and Bitfinex to provide customer KYC files, IP logs, linked bank accounts and complete transaction histories. A federal police report traced funds from "Team Libra Wallets" across multiple blockchains and centralized exchanges, including transfers allegedly structured to complicate tracking. The case stems from President Javier Milei's promotion of the LIBRA token on X in February 2025. — link

Binance will apply its Seed Tag to AERO. Binance has postponed the launch of Aerodrome (AERO) spot trading from 11:00 UTC to 16:00 UTC on July 17. text: Token Buybacks Cannot Rescue Weak Valuations, Ansem Says Crypto trader Ansem said token buybacks do not necessarily provide lasting support for valuations, pointing to the sharp gap between Hyperliquid and Pump.fun. The two platforms generate about USD 800 million and USD 440 million in annualized revenue, respectively, and both use part of their profits to repurchase tokens, yet HYPE carries a fully diluted valuation of roughly USD 65 billion versus about USD 1.4 billion for PUMP. Ansem arguing that Hyperliquid consistently rewards core users while Pump.fun's still-unfulfilled airdrop promise has weakened its "trust premium." — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: USDT Trading in Venezuela Now Rivals the Country's Oil Exports Between June 11 and July 13, about 1.389 billion USDT was traded on Binance's peer-to-peer market in Venezuela, averaging roughly USD 44 million a day. Ecoanalítica, a Venezuelan economic research firm, estimated that the volume was equivalent to about 75% of the country's monthly oil exports and represented a significant share of the central bank's foreign-currency supply in June. USDT's local over-the-counter price has risen to about 840 bolivars, roughly 15.5% above the official exchange rate. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Highlight Clip Fed Chair: We're Not Bailing Out Anybody, Including Crypto Fed Chair: We're Not Bailing Out Anybody, Including Crypto Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said at a July 14 hearing of the U.S. House Financial Services Committee that the Fed does not want to be in the bailout business, and hopes reforms can reduce extreme risks so the financial system is not in a position where anyone needs to be bailed out, including the crypto industry. However, when pressed on whether the Fed would provide support if stablecoins or the crypto market faced a run, Warsh did not make a clear commitment that there would be no bailout under any circumstances. — link
